originalTitle: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust
summary: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short warranty. "I think the most important thing is trust," Migicovsky told me in an interview this week. "Do people trust the product […]
content: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they’re signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short warranty. “I think the most important thing is trust,” Migicovsky told me in an interview this week. “Do people trust the product that we’re making and do they trust the company and the people behind it?” Migicovsky revived Pebble early last year, after the original company shut down in 2016. The new generation of Pebbles has the same aesthetic, but with updated tech, including larger e-paper displays. I was excited about Pebble’s return after missing out on the originals, but hesitated after noticing their 30-day warranty. That’s about as long as the Pebble Time 2’s battery life, so you might not even need to charge it before it’s no longer covered. While I ended up ordering a Pebble Round 2 that I’m still waiting for, others have also raised concerns about the watches’ warranty.  People have also reported running into hardware issues early on, like the front glass cracking on the Pebble Time 2. Earlier this week, Migicovsky addressed the issues in a blog post, saying Pebble has replaced 330 Time 2 watches for free so far, out of over 19,000 watches “in the field.”  Pebble’s blog says it will “continue replacing reasonable reports of glass cracking for free as long as we can. At some point, we will shift to offering a replacement at a highly discounted amount.” Additionally, Pebble is “looking into” offering replacement parts so people can do DIY repairs on their watches.  While Migicovsky hasn’t offered a timeline for when a shift might happen, he emphasized to The Verge that “an immensely small percentage” of watches have had this issue, adding, “It’s, for all intents and purposes, no skin off our back whatsoever to just offer straight up free replacements for anyone who has a problem.”  “At every step of the way we’ve communicated, over-communicated about what to expect [and] what the product’s going to be like.” Pebble warned customers in its announcement for the Pebble 2 Duo and Pebble Time 2 that they shouldn’t buy one if they need a “perfectly polished smartwatch,” noting that “Things could not last as long as you’d like.”  “This is effectively a grassroots, bottoms-up relaunch of something that we love, and really wanted to see exist in the world,” Migicovsky told me. “In terms of how we support the product, there’s just certain things that we could sign up to do, and there’s certain things that we couldn’t.” “You can expect a product that’s going to work great, and something that will make you smile, but we can’t guarantee it for maybe the length that other companies or other consumer product companies could with their supply chains and networks of stores all around the world to help with replacements. We just don’t have that.”   “It’s a Pebble, through and through,” Migicovsky says. “If you enjoyed the first go around, you’re going to love the second go around. But that having been said, it’s not exactly the same as the first era.”
chineseTitle: Pebble 创始人 Eric Migicovsky 表示，他的 30 天保修完全取决于信任
chineseSummary: Pebble 创始人埃里克·米吉科夫斯基 (Eric Migicovsky) 表示，其新款电子纸智能手表的购买者应该知道他们要购买什么，并相信 Pebble 会在遇到问题时解决问题，尽管保修期很短。 “我认为最重要的是信任，”米吉科夫斯基在本周的一次采访中告诉我。 “人们信任该产品吗[…]
chineseContent: Pebble 创始人埃里克·米吉科夫斯基 (Eric Migicovsky) 表示，其新型电子纸智能手表的购买者应该知道他们要购买什么，并相信 Pebble 会在遇到问题时解决问题，尽管保修期很短。 “我认为最重要的是信任，”米吉科夫斯基在本周的一次采访中告诉我。 “人们是否信任我们正在生产的产品以及公司及其背后的人员？”在原公司于 2016 年关闭后，Migicovsky 去年年初重新启动了 Pebble。新一代 Pebble 具有相同的美感，但采用了更新的技术，包括更大的电子纸显示屏。在错过了原版后，我对 Pebble 的回归感到兴奋，但在注意到他们的 30 天保修后犹豫了。这大约与 Pebble Time 2 的电池寿命一样长，因此您甚至可能不需要在它不再被覆盖之前为其充电。虽然我最终订购了一块仍在等待的 Pebble Round 2，但其他人也对手表的保修提出了担忧。  人们还报告说，他们很早就遇到了硬件问题，比如 Pebble Time 2 的前玻璃破裂。本周早些时候，Migicovsky 在一篇博客文章中解决了这些问题，称到目前为止，Pebble 已经免费更换了 330 块 Time 2 手表，其中“现场”有超过 19,000 块手表。  Pebble 的博客表示，它将“尽可能继续免费更换有关玻璃破裂的合理报告。在某个时候，我们将转向以高折扣金额提供更换品。”此外，Pebble 正在“研究”提供替换零件，以便人们可以对手表进行 DIY 维修。  虽然 Migicovsky 没有提供何时可能发生这种转变的时间表，但他向 The Verge 强调，“极少数”的手表出现了这个问题，并补充道：“对于所有手表来说，这是一个非常小的问题。”
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
